aesthetic: American diamond brilliance, architectural jewelry precision, Tiffany Blue as a cultural icon, craft legacy anchored in the 1837 Tiffany Setting with contemporary energy introduced under LVMH — celebratory, confident, and emotionally resonant
clientele: milestone-driven women and couples 25-55 who associate Tiffany with life's most significant moments; a growing younger segment 22-38 entering fine jewelry through T and HardWear collections as a statement of personal taste, not occasion
brand_taboos: heavy baroque styling without diamond quality narrative
clienteling_tone: celebratory yet measured, knowledgeable about stone quality and craft heritage, personally significant rather than transactional — the Blue Box is the beginning of a story, not the end of a sale
